stem: A network performance study measures signal latency for 25 samples to determine if the process variability is below 50ms for 99% of packets. The sample standard deviation is 3.31 ms (sample variance = 10.96). A second measurement method on 20 samples gives a sample variance of 7.43 (SD = 2.73 ms). The two methods may have different measurement precision.  Estimate the true population standard deviation of signal latency and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Method 1: s=3.31, 95% CI for σ = [2.58, 4.61]. Method 2: s=2.73, 95% CI for σ = [2.07, 3.98]. Combined interval: [2.07, 4.61]. Point estimate: 3.03 ms.

solution_steps: Combined interval accounting for method uncertainty: [2.07, 4.61].
common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (3.31) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
Averaging the two sample variances without considering they may represent different measurement precisions
